title: Disjoint Sets, Connectivity, and Offline Grouping · Correctness Defense
summary: Track changing network connectivity across merges without scanning every member. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a union-find service with measured compression. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent disjoint sets as parent forests with explicit representative invariants.
Apply union by rank or size without corrupting representative metadata.
Trace path compression and explain its interaction with later finds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
